vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1941

plan.title: feat(cli): directive migrate (stage-2 canonical-vendored -> npm-hybrid) -- thin provenance verb

narratives.Description: The legacy-layout migration to 1.0 is a two-stage bridge (#1912). Stage 1 (frozen Go installer) normalizes any old on-disk layout to canonical .deft/core/. Stage 2 -- this story -- is a NEW top-level TS verb `directive migrate` (alias `deft migrate`) that only ever covers canonical-vendored -> npm-hybrid: detect a canonical-vendored .deft/core deposit, stamp its VERSION/manifest as npm-managed (the handshake sentinel doctor/update recognize), verify the global engine resolves (signpost the README if absent, never install), idempotent + safe with a timestamped backup, three-state exit. Per the locked F decision the on-disk shape is UNCHANGED this wave -- this is a thin provenance verb, not a content engine. It blocks the #1912 freeze as prerequisite (b) and contributes the stage-2 leg of the pinned legacy -> stage-1 -> stage-2 -> npm-hybrid e2e.
narratives.Overview:
## What to build

A new top-level `directive migrate` (alias `deft migrate`) verb in the TS engine.

### Does
1. Detect a canonical-vendored `.deft/core` deposit (the vendored install state; reuse the manifest locator in packages/core/src/doctor/manifest.ts).
2. Stamp provenance: rewrite `.deft/core/VERSION` / the install manifest to mark it npm-managed (a handshake sentinel field doctor / future `directive update` recognize), so npm owns the payload.
3. Verify the global engine resolves (`@deftai/directive` on PATH / resolvable via packages/core/src/content-root.ts resolveContentPackageRoot). If absent, signpost the README (`npm i -g ...`) -- do NOT attempt an install (#1933 never-first-start decree).
4. Idempotent + safe: no-op when already hybrid (sentinel present); write a timestamped backup before any rewrite.
5. Three-state exit: 0 migrated/already-hybrid · 1 needs action (e.g. engine missing -> README) · 2 config error.

### Does NOT
- Normalize legacy layouts (stage-1 frozen Go guarantees canonical-vendored input).
- Move / rename / trim `.deft/core` content (F: shape unchanged this wave).
- Download any payload (npm / `directive update` own content delivery).

## Integration points
- packages/cli/src/cli-router/route-argv.ts: add `migrate` to TOP_LEVEL_UX_VERBS (the #1670 locked vocabulary) + a routeTopLevel branch routing `directive migrate` -> dispatch ["migrate", ...rest]; `deft migrate` resolves identically.
- packages/cli/src/dispatch.ts: wire the `migrate` verb to a runMigrate handler the same way `init`/`update` are dispatched (dedicated branch, NOT a CLI_MODULE_VERBS entry unless that matches the init/update pattern); update any help/contract verb list.
- Reuse the installer's safe-rewrite + timestamped-backup conventions used by the manifest writes.

NOTE: distinct from the legacy `task migrate:vbrief` (pre-v0.20 document-model migration, Python). This is the npm provenance verb.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- packages/core/src/init-deposit/migrate.ts (new): detectCanonicalVendored(projectRoot) using doctor/manifest.ts locateManifest/parseManifest; stampNpmManaged(manifestPath) with timestamped backup; engine-resolve check via content-root.ts resolveContentPackageRoot; orchestrator returning a three-state result + structured reason.
- packages/cli/src/init-cli/migrate.ts (new): runMigrate(argv, io) CLI handler that calls the core orchestrator, prints the README signpost on engine-absent, and maps the result to the 0/1/2 exit code.
- packages/cli/src/cli-router/route-argv.ts: register `migrate` in TOP_LEVEL_UX_VERBS + routeTopLevel.
- packages/cli/src/dispatch.ts: dispatch `migrate` to runMigrate.
- Tests (packages/core/src/init-deposit/migrate.test.ts + packages/cli/src/init-cli/migrate.test.ts + route-argv.test.ts): canonical-vendored fixture -> migrate -> assert provenance flip + idempotent no-op on second run; engine-present vs engine-absent (README signpost) branches; three-state exit; routing of `directive migrate` / `deft migrate`.
narratives.UserStory: As an operator on a canonical-vendored Deft install, I want `directive migrate` to stamp my `.deft/core` as npm-managed (idempotently, with a backup, no content move, no download), so that the npm channel cleanly owns my payload and `directive update` takes over upgrades.

narrative.Acceptance: When directive migrate runs on a canonical-vendored .deft/core, it rewrites VERSION/manifest with the npm-managed sentinel and writes a timestamped backup first.
When migrate runs a second time on an already-npm-managed deposit, it makes no change and exits 0 (already-hybrid).
When the global engine does not resolve, migrate prints the README npm signpost and exits 1 (needs action) without attempting any install or download.

items.title: no content move and no payload download

narrative.Acceptance: When migrate runs, it does not move/rename/trim .deft/core content and does not download any payload (shape unchanged per decision F).

items.title: migrate verb routes from directive and deft

narrative.Acceptance: When a user runs `directive migrate` or `deft migrate`, the router dispatches the runMigrate handler (migrate registered in the #1670 top-level vocabulary).
When migrate completes, it returns 0 (migrated/already-hybrid), 1 (needs action), or 2 (config error), each covered by a unit test.

references.title: Issue #1912: frozen Go binary -- freeze prerequisite (b)

expected_outputs: directive/deft migrate stamps canonical-vendored .deft/core npm-managed, idempotent, with backup; no content move/download
three-state exit; engine-absent README signpost; verb registered in #1670 vocabulary
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Traced to GitHub issue #1941 (scope) + #1912 / #1933 / #1670 locked decisions and the existing doctor/manifest.ts + content-root.ts surfaces, not a spec section.
